description: Polish the existing one-click 'Send Reminders' feature on the host dashboard so it uses a branded React Email template, tracks when each guest was last reminded, and provides clear inline UI feedback instead of browser-native dialogs.

userStories.title: Create a polished React Email reminder template
userStories.description: As a host, I want reminder emails to look as professional as invitation emails so that guests take the reminder seriously and RSVP.
acceptanceCriteria: A new React Email component exists at src/lib/email-templates/reminder.tsx exporting renderReminderHtml and renderReminderText (following the pattern in src/lib/email-templates/invitation.tsx)
The reminder template includes: guest name, event title, date, time, location, cover image (if set), host name, and a prominent 'RSVP Now' CTA button linking to /rsvp/[token]
The template uses the Shindig brand color (#7c3aed) and the same table-based layout as the invitation template for email-client compatibility
The subject line is 'Reminder: RSVP for {eventTitle}'
A plain-text version is generated alongside the HTML version
The remind API route at src/app/api/events/manage/[id]/remind/route.ts is updated to use the new template instead of the inline HTML from src/lib/emailTemplates.ts
TypeScript strict-mode typecheck passes (npm run build)
E2E test in e2e/api-invite.spec.ts verifies the remind endpoint still returns 401 without auth

userStories.title: Track when each guest was last reminded
userStories.description: As a host, I want the system to record when a reminder was last sent to each guest so that I can see who has been reminded and avoid spamming guests with duplicate reminders.
acceptanceCriteria: A new migration adds a nullable reminded_at timestamptz column to the guests table (default null)
The remind API route (src/app/api/events/manage/[id]/remind/route.ts) sets reminded_at = now() for each guest after successfully sending their reminder email
The v1 remind API route (src/app/api/v1/events/[id]/remind/route.ts) also sets reminded_at on success
The Guest type in src/lib/types.ts includes the reminded_at field as string | null
The GuestList component (src/components/GuestList.tsx) shows a 'Reminded' indicator (e.g. relative timestamp or dot) on guests whose reminded_at is set, visible in the Pending filter tab
TypeScript strict-mode typecheck passes (npm run build)
E2E test verifies that after seeding a guest with reminded_at set, the dashboard guest list displays the reminded indicator

userStories.title: Improve dashboard remind UX with inline confirmation and feedback
userStories.description: As a host, I want to see how many guests will be reminded before confirming, and receive clear inline feedback after sending, so that the one-click experience feels polished and trustworthy.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Send Reminders' ActionButton on the dashboard (src/app/dashboard/[id]/page.tsx) displays the count of pending guests with email addresses, e.g. 'Send Reminders (5)'
Clicking the button opens a styled confirmation dialog (not window.confirm) showing the count and a list summary, with 'Send' and 'Cancel' buttons
After sending, an inline toast or banner replaces window.alert, showing 'Sent X reminders' on success or 'Sent X, Y failed' if some failed, auto-dismissing after 5 seconds
The button shows a loading/spinner state while the API request is in flight
The button remains disabled when there are no pending guests with email addresses
TypeScript strict-mode typecheck passes (npm run build)
E2E test on the dashboard page verifies the reminder button shows the pending count and is disabled when no guests are pending
